name: Get Max Payout Amount
summary: Funds endpoint to retrieve the maximum payout amount available for a logged-in user's account. A POST call with jData (uid, actid) and jKey returns the account id and the maximum payout amount.

request_params.name: jData*
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list

json_fields.name: uid
json_fields.description: User id of the logged in user.

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a successful response.

notes: The "FUNDS" section begins on PDF page 64 with the GET MAX PAYOUT AMOUNT endpoint only. The subsequent ALL-CAPS titles (FUNDS PAYOUT REQUEST, GET PAYIN REPORT) on page 65 are separate endpoints outside this focal section and were excluded. Both jData fields (uid, actid) are mandatory (trailing '*'). The jData JSON is sent URL-form-encoded alongside jKey (jData={...}&jKey=...) per the Noren/PiConnect convention. In the cleaned-text success response a stray page number "112" appears between "request_time" and "stat" (an OCR/pagination artifact); it was removed from the reconstructed sample since it is not part of the JSON (the page image confirms a clean JSON object). No "Possible value" enum cells were populated for any request or response field in the source table.
issues: Draft hallucinated an 'emsg' response field that is NOT in the page-64 RESPONSE DETAILS table (emsg appears only in the sample failure JSON, never in the response-fields table) — removed. The cleaned page-64 success-response text contains a stray "112" page-number artifact mid-JSON; the reconstructed sample omits it (the image confirms a clean JSON object). All remaining field/possible-value/description bindings cross-checked against the page-64 image and are unambiguous; no possible-value enum cells exist for this section.
